data.title: Economist Peter Schiff Warns of Potential Market Impact from US-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Economist Peter Schiff has issued a warning regarding the potential market impact if the conflict between the United States and Iran escalates and prolongs. According to NS3.AI, Schiff suggests that stocks, bonds, cryptocurrencies, and the US dollar could experience declines, while gold and oil prices might surge. Schiff believes that current market pricing assumes a brief and successful conflict, a scenario he deems unlikely.

In a separate analysis, Rashad Hajiyev, an analyst, forecasts that gold prices could rise significantly, reaching between $7,000 and $8,000 in the future. Hajiyev attributes this potential increase to the beginning of a new advancing cycle in precious metals and mining stocks.

data.title: Analysts: The Federal Reserve is caught in a dilemma due to the latest jobs report deviating from expectations.
data.description: Brian Jacobsen, chief economist at Annex Wealth Management, said the non-farm payroll data was disappointing and deviated significantly from expectations. The report's data cannot be embellished; the rising unemployment rate, coupled with a sharp increase in oil prices, will raise concerns among traders about the risk of stagflation. The Federal Reserve will face a dilemma: should it take measures to help the job market, or maintain the status quo (or even threaten to raise interest rates) to curb inflation expectations? (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Military Plans to Ensure Safe Passage for Ships
data.description: The White House National Economic Council Director, Kevin Hassett, announced that the U.S. military is formulating a plan to ensure the safe passage of ships. According to Jin10, this development comes amid rising geopolitical tensions that could potentially impact global trade routes. The initiative aims to safeguard maritime navigation and maintain stability in international waters. Details of the plan have not been disclosed, but it underscores the U.S. commitment to protecting its interests and allies in key regions. The move is part of broader efforts to address security challenges and ensure the free flow of commerce across the seas.

data.title: U.S. White House Economic Adviser Comments on Iran and Oil Issues
data.description: U.S. White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett stated that there will be no immediate discussions on utilizing the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) in relation to Iran and oil issues. According to Jin10, this announcement comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and fluctuating oil markets. The decision reflects the current administration's stance on managing national energy resources without resorting to emergency reserves. This approach aims to maintain stability in the energy sector while addressing international concerns.

data.title: Target Faces Challenges Amid Bonus Cuts
data.description: Target is reducing employee bonuses, marking another difficult period for the retailer. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's recent struggles. Despite these challenges, there is potential for improvement in the future. The Retail Monitor provides further insights into the situation.

data.title: Strategy released details of its top 10 asset management company holdings: Vanguard holds 8.12%, Morgan Stanley holds 2.08%.
data.description: Bitcoin treasury company Strategy released its top ten holdings in global asset management companies on the X platform, including: 1. The Vanguard Group, 8.12% stake, valued at $3.183 billion; 2. Capital Research & Management, 7.7% stake, valued at $3.019 billion; 3. BlackRock Fund Advisors, 3.64% stake, valued at $1.428 billion; 4. Capital Research & Management, 2.62% stake, valued at $1.026 billion; 5. SSGA Funds Management, 2.29% stake, valued at $897 million; 6. Morgan Stanley, 2.08% stake, valued at $815 million; 7. UBS Securities, 2.02% stake, valued at $793 million; 8. Amundi Asset Management SASU, 1.77% stake, valued at $693 million. 9. Geode Capital Management holds 1.46% of the shares, with a market value of $573 million; 10. Norges Bank Investment Management holds 1.32% of the shares, with a market value of $517 million.

data.title: Global Markets Show Resilience Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
The current global economic situation is markedly different from the turmoil experienced in 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the markets have shown resilience despite ongoing geopolitical tensions. Analysts suggest that while challenges remain, the financial landscape is not as volatile as it was during the previous crisis.

Experts note that the current conditions are influenced by various factors, including policy adjustments and strategic responses from governments and financial institutions. These measures have contributed to stabilizing the markets, preventing a repeat of the severe disruptions seen last year.

While uncertainties persist, particularly in regions affected by geopolitical conflicts, the overall outlook remains cautiously optimistic. Market participants are closely monitoring developments and adjusting strategies to navigate potential risks.

The situation underscores the importance of adaptive strategies and proactive measures in maintaining economic stability amid global challenges. As the world continues to grapple with geopolitical issues, the resilience of financial markets will be crucial in shaping future economic trajectories.

data.title: Gate Ventures announced a strategic investment in Utexo to drive the development of Bitcoin-native stablecoin infrastructure.
data.description: Gate Ventures, the venture capital arm of cryptocurrency exchange Gate, has officially announced a strategic investment in Utexo. Utexo is a Bitcoin-native settlement infrastructure that integrates the RGB protocol and the Lightning Network, enabling private, instant, and non-custodial stablecoin trading on the Bitcoin network. It directly introduces stablecoin liquidity into the Bitcoin ecosystem and expands decentralized finance (DeFi) applications. This collaboration demonstrates Gate Ventures' continued support for infrastructure development, aiming to strengthen Bitcoin's role in the digital financial system. By enabling native stablecoin issuance, payments, and trading on Bitcoin, Utexo leverages Bitcoin's security while ensuring privacy and self-custody. Its architecture also provides the foundation for Bitcoin-native financial applications, including decentralized exchanges, payment networks, and scalable settlement systems, offering easy access for developers, wallets, and financial institutions.

data.title: Lithuanian Prosecutors Charge Five in Alleged Terror Plot Linked to Russian Intelligence
data.description: Lithuanian authorities have charged five individuals with orchestrating and executing terrorist activities allegedly on behalf of Russian military intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X that the suspects are accused of attempting to place incendiary devices on aircraft. The charges highlight ongoing tensions and security concerns in the region, as Lithuania continues to address threats linked to foreign entities. The case underscores the broader geopolitical challenges faced by the Baltic nation, which has been vigilant in countering espionage and sabotage efforts. The investigation is part of Lithuania's broader strategy to safeguard national security and maintain stability amid regional uncertainties.

data.title: U.S. Economy's Resilience to Energy Shocks Examined
data.description: The U.S. economy's current resilience to energy shocks is under examination. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the nation's energy production capabilities have evolved significantly. This shift has altered the economy's sensitivity to fluctuations in energy prices. Analysts suggest that the increased domestic energy production has provided a buffer against potential disruptions, reducing the impact of global energy market volatility on the U.S. economy. This development marks a departure from previous decades when energy shocks had more pronounced effects on economic stability.

data.title: Oil-Rich Regions in the U.S. Experience Economic Growth
data.description: Economic growth is being observed in oil-rich regions of the United States, such as Texas, Western Pennsylvania, and North Dakota. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic benefits these areas are experiencing due to their oil resources. The increased activity in the oil sector is contributing to local economies, providing jobs and boosting regional development. This growth is particularly significant as it contrasts with economic challenges faced in other parts of the country. The oil industry continues to play a crucial role in these regions, driving economic progress and stability.

data.title: Emerging Market Currencies Hit Intraday Low Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description: Emerging market currencies experienced a decline, reaching an intraday low following comments from U.S. President Donald Trump. According to Jin10, President Trump stated that no agreement would be made with Iran unless it surrenders, escalating tensions between the two nations. This development has contributed to increased volatility in the foreign exchange markets, particularly affecting emerging market currencies. The geopolitical uncertainty surrounding U.S.-Iran relations continues to impact investor sentiment and market stability.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly: No single month's data is decisive.
data.description: The US non-farm payrolls report for February unexpectedly came in negative territory, and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%. Federal Reserve Chairman Daly stated that no single month's data is decisive; employment data is neither a clear nor a false signal. (Jinshi)

data.title: Treasuries Rise Amid Anticipation of Federal Reserve Rate Cuts
data.description: Treasuries experienced gains following a disappointing payroll report, which has heightened expectations for interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year. Bloomberg posted on X that despite the recent increase in oil prices, which poses a risk of fueling inflation, the market is optimistic about potential rate reductions. The payroll data has shifted investor sentiment, leading to increased demand for Treasuries as the likelihood of monetary easing grows. This development comes amid ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures driven by rising energy costs.

data.title: Expert Warns of Potential Delays in Fed Rate Cuts Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Goldman Sachs' Lindsay Rosner, head of multi-asset fixed income, has highlighted concerns over the U.S. labor market's weakness, which could prompt the Federal Reserve to delay interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, Rosner noted that ongoing Middle East conflicts, particularly developments in Iran, are overshadowing U.S. employment conditions and complicating the path to policy normalization. Despite these challenges, Rosner anticipates that the Fed will eventually proceed with the remaining two 'normalization rate cuts' to bring interest rates back to a neutral level. However, the timing remains uncertain due to the current geopolitical uncertainties.

data.title: Beauty Market Experiences Significant Growth During Pandemic
data.description: The beauty industry has witnessed remarkable growth during the pandemic, as people spent more time at home focusing on self-care and skincare routines. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the surge in demand for beauty products as consumers adapted to new lifestyle changes. This trend reflects a broader shift in consumer behavior, with many prioritizing personal wellness and self-care amid global uncertainties. The beauty market's expansion during this period underscores its resilience and adaptability in the face of challenging circumstances.

data.title: Traders now estimate the probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in June to have risen to about 50%.
data.description: Traders now estimate the probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in June to have risen to about 50%, up from just 35% before the jobs data release. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: The US labor market experiences a sudden "cold snap," with February's non-farm payrolls unexpectedly recording negative growth.
data.description: U.S. employers unexpectedly cut jobs in February, and the unemployment rate rose, raising questions about the health of the labor market. Nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000 last month, after a strong start to the year, according to data released Friday by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS). The unemployment rate climbed to 4.4%. The decline in employment partly reflects a reduction in healthcare jobs due to strikes. Following the data release, spot gold and silver rose across the board, as traders increased bets on at least one more rate cut by the Federal Reserve in 2026. (Golden Ten)

data.title: Incident Reported North of Oman in Hormuz Strait
data.description: An incident has been reported six nautical miles north of Oman in the Hormuz Strait, according to Jin10. The UK Maritime Trade Operations office has confirmed the occurrence, though details remain sparse. The Hormuz Strait is a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments, and any disruptions in this area can have significant implications for international trade and energy markets. Further information is awaited as authorities investigate the situation.

data.title: U.S. February Non-Farm Payrolls Decline by 92,000, Missing Expectations
data.description: The U.S. labor market experienced a setback in February as non-farm payrolls decreased by 92,000, marking the first negative value since October 2025. According to Jin10, this decline contrasts sharply with market expectations, which had anticipated an increase of 59,000 jobs. The unexpected drop in employment figures raises concerns about the strength of the U.S. economic recovery and could influence future monetary policy decisions. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments to assess their potential impact on the broader economy.
